description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rugged, angular form and a mix of pink, white, and dark gray coloration. The surface is rough and uneven, with visible mineral banding and patches of different textures. One side features a small, rectangular label with numbers, suggesting it may have been cataloged or collected for study. The rock's coloration is dominated by pinkish hues interspersed with streaks of white and dark gray, giving it a striking, variegated appearance. The overall form is blocky with sharp edges and some flat faces, typical of a rock that has fractured naturally. Its size and mass suggest it could serve as a physical barrier or obstacle in an urban environment, such as a park or landscaped area.
description: A rugged, angular rock with pink, white, and dark gray banding, featuring a rough, blocky surface and a catalog label on one side.
description_view_0: This view highlights the pink and white banding with a prominent rough texture and angular edges.
description_view_1: This side shows a dark gray patch and a visible catalog label, contrasting with the lighter mineral streaks.
description_view_2: The rock appears more blocky from this angle, with a mix of dark and pink surfaces and less visible banding.
description_view_3: This view emphasizes the layered appearance of the rock, with pink and white stripes running across a rough face.
